evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: We move quickly, we're visible, and we don't accept "this is how we've always done it."
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Velocity language ("move quickly", "short cycles", "seeing your work land in the real world fast") appears alongside genuine rigour signals (data contracts, modelling standards, data quality/reliability), giving rough parity, so mixed.
evidence.domain_risk: makes Bravida's operational and financial data trustworthy, consistent, and accessible
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Financial and operational data underpins business decisions at scale but the JD language is about trustworthiness and consistency rather than regulatory/compliance/audit exposure, keeping this at moderate.
evidence.data_team_maturity: The Data & Analytics team sits inside Operational Excellence, a fast-moving function
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named tools in active responsibilities (dbt, Snowflake, Dagster) and an existing semantic layer with room to grow toward generative BI indicate a function that exists and is scaling, not built from zero nor fully mature with sub-teams.
evidence.jd_authorship: Architect and own data models in dbt on Snowflake as the primary deliverable
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Named tools with precise application context (dbt on Snowflake, Dagster pipelines, data contracts, semantic layer) show clear technical specificity from someone who has done this job.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Work directly with operations, procurement, and finance to model their domains properly
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Operations, procurement, and finance are named as equally weighted domains the role models directly, spanning commercial/internal_data/finance categories with genuinely split emphasis.
evidence.autonomy_level: Architect and own data models in dbt on Snowflake as the primary deliverable
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Verbs like "architect and own", "define and enforce", and "drive our move toward generative analytics" clearly set direction and priorities rather than execute scoped work.
evidence.ai_role: Work with AI tooling actively to accelerate your own output and enable smarter self-service for the business
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate uses AI tools to accelerate their own work (ai_user signal) but is also expected to build the semantic layer and infrastructure that generative BI/natural-language querying consumes, which per the tie-breaker classifies as ai_enabler.
evidence.testing_framing: Define and enforce modelling standards, documentation, and data contracts across domains
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data contracts and quality/reliability improvements are framed as something the candidate defines and enforces with clear ownership verbs, not merely a listed tool.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Lead analytics data quality and reliability improvements across the stack
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Reliability and trustworthiness are named concerns but secondary to the delivery/ownership framing dominating the JD, with no regulatory or compliance language, matching moderate.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Drive our move toward generative analytics, shaping how business users query and interpret data through natural language
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role owns new greenfield territory (generative BI, natural language querying) while also enforcing standards and improving reliability across an existing stack, a genuine mix.
evidence.language_gate: English required; Nordic languages a bonus
evidence.loss_aversion: Lead analytics data quality and reliability improvements across the stack — reflects operational reliability concern rather than compliance or regulatory exposure.
